title: WHISTLER
author: Ann Patchett
description: Decades after their time together, a woman reconnects with her stepfather and they look back at the directions their lives have taken.

title: THEO OF GOLDEN
author: Allen Levi
description: A man travels to a small Southern town, where he purchases pencil drawings of local residents and exchanges them for stories.

title: THE DEAL
author: Elle Kennedy
description: Hannah Wells makes a deal with the captain of the college hockey team in exchange for tutoring him that may complicate their relationship.

title: YESTERYEAR
author: Caro Claire Burke
description: Natalie Heller Mills, a privileged tradwife social media influencer, wakes up to find she is living in the comparatively difficult reality of 1855.

title: THE DIVORCE
author: Freida McFadden
description: After her husband ends their marriage and she turns her attention to his new girlfriend, Naomi suspects she might be in danger.

title: VIEW FROM THE EAST WING
author: Jill Biden
description: The former first lady recounts her time in the White House, causes she championed and her simultaneous work as a community college professor.

title: REVOLUTION
author: Eric Metaxas
description: The author of “Martin Luther” and “Bonhoeffer” gives an account of the foundation of the United States of America.

title: STRANGERS
author: Belle Burden
description: Burden retraces her marriage of 20 years in search of clues to help shape her understanding about its demise and to find a way forward.

title: THE LAND AND ITS PEOPLE
author: David Sedaris
description: Essays on the passage of time, complicated relationships and some unexpected delights life has to offer.

title: THE FIX
author: Barbara McQuade
description: The former U.S. attorney for the Eastern District of Michigan describes what she considers threats to American democracy and national security.

title: WONDER
author: R.J. Palacio
description: A boy with a facial deformity starts school. (Ages 8 to 12)

title: WOMBAT WAITING
author: Katherine Applegate
description: A homeless pup patiently waits for her “special person.” (Ages 8 to 12)

title: REFUGEE
author: Alan Gratz
description: Three children in three different conflicts look for safe haven. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: KPOP DEMON HUNTERS
author: Jessica Yoon
description: Huntrix takes on the Saja Boys, a boy band who happen to be demons in disguise. Based on the movie. (Ages 7 to 10)

title: UNSETTLING SALAD!
author: Aaron Reynolds. Illustrated Peter Brown
description: Thadeus and Oliver take a bite out of a salad that may bite back! (Ages 6 to 9)

title: SING THE 50 UNITED STATES!
author: Dr. Seuss
description: A song that helps children remember the names of the 50 states of America. (Ages 3 to 7)

title: AMERICA, I'M SO GLAD YOU WERE BORN
author: Ainsley Earhardt. Illustrated Kim Barnes
description: A celebration of America’s birthday. (Ages 4 to 8)

title: WHY A DAUGHTER NEEDS A DAD
author: Gregory E. Lang. Illustrated Sydney Hanson
description: A father's love for his daughter. (Ages 4 to 8)

title: I LOVE DAD WITH THE VERY HUNGRY CATERPILLAR
author: Eric Carle
description: Celebrating dad with help from that insatiable insect. (Ages 3 to 5)

title: WHY A SON NEEDS A DAD
author: Gregory E. Lang. Illustrated Gail Yerrill
description: The bond between father and son. (Ages 4 to 7)

title: DIARY OF A WIMPY KID
author: Jeff Kinney. Illustrated Jeff Kinney
description: The travails and challenges of adolescence. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: THE HUNGER GAMES
author: Suzanne Collins
description: In a dystopia, a girl fights for survival on live TV. (Ages 12 and up)

title: THE WILD ROBOT
author: Peter Brown
description: Roz the robot adapts to her surroundings on a remote, wild island. (Ages 7 to 12)

title: WINGS OF FIRE
author: Tui T. Sutherland
description: Only the five dragonets of destiny can unite the seven warring dragon tribes. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: PERCY JACKSON & THE OLYMPIANS
author: Rick Riordan
description: A boy battles mythological monsters. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: THE SECRET WORLD OF BRIAR ROSE
author: Cindy Pham
description: Two sisters discover an underground, magical dreamworld. (Ages 14 and up)

title: THE HEIRS
author: Faridah Àbíké-Íyímídé
description: The five adopted children of the billionaire Leontes Button are suspects in his murder. (Ages 14 to 18)

title: THE ESCAPE GAME
author: Marissa Meyer and Tamara Moss
description: A real murder takes place on an escape room-themed game show. (Ages 14 and up)

title: FAKE SKATING
author: Lynn Painter
description: After years of separation, the childhood friends Alec and Dani reunite and face their true feelings toward each other. (Ages 14 and up)

title: SHADOW REAPER
author: Lynette Noni
description: Viridia, a reaper hunter, and Reeve, a reaper, join forces to stop the Reaper Priest’s plot to destroy the city of Aravell. (Ages 14 and up)

title: THE LET THEM THEORY
author: Mel Robbins
description: Read by the author. 10 hours, 38 minutes unabridged.

title: ATOMIC HABITS
author: James Clear
description: Read by the author. 5 hours, 35 minutes unabridged.

title: BIRTH VIBES
author: Jen Hamilton
description: Read by the author. 11 hours, 15 minutes unabridged.

title: THE 48 LAWS OF POWER
author: Robert Greene
description: Read by Richard Poe. 23 hours, 6 minutes unabridged.

title: THE MOUNTAIN IS YOU
author: Brianna Wiest
description: Read by Stacey Glemboski. 5 hours, 44 minutes unabridged.
